goal: Implement a broad live runtime preview system: classify all 341 scalar rows and 7 structured families, build the reversible runtime preview executor with session/throttle/dead-man models, prove a live apply-and-revert round trip, and surface readiness in the UI.

liveRoundTripProof.result: proven: session captured original 5 5 5 5, applied preview 9 live, verified via read-only getoption, reverted, verified restoration byte-for-byte
defectsCaughtByLiveProof: css_gap values must be rendered as an integer or a top/right/bottom/left Lua table; the quoted-string form is rejected by hl.config (fixed with full CSS shorthand mapping)
hyprctl eval reports evaluation errors on stdout with exit status 0; the runner now treats error output as failure so reverts are never falsely assumed

sourceGuards: runtime preview modules contain no config-file write APIs and no active config path references
runtime preview modules contain no reload invocation; constructed commands are proven to never contain a reload form
slider ticks issue at most one throttled runtime set and never a config write (throttle model tested)
unsupported, blocked, and not-proven rows are rejected before any command is built
dead-man rows are rejected without a confirmed dead-man session and are disabled by default
saving marks the session and defers persistence to the existing backup/write/reread config path exactly once
every session captures the original value read-only before any mutation and carries a revert path
the UI readiness card is display-only with an insensitive control; blocked rows expose no enabled preview control
testsAdded: live_gaps_in_preview_applies_and_reverts (ignored, env-gated, run once this sprint: passed)
nextRecommendedWork: Per-setting controls and the supervised dead-man UI are wired. Next: per-row live-proof runs for the 63 needs-live-proof input/cursor rows.
